notes.note: The Kalends were the first day of the Roman month, so the kalends of June is 1 June (44 BC). Brutus and Cassius fear the veterans mustering at Rome will swell by that date.
Caesar's discharged soldiers, settled on colonial land grants and fiercely loyal to his memory; their massed presence at Rome made them a standing political threat to the assassins.

An altar and column had been raised on the spot in the Forum where Caesar's body was cremated; the consuls had it removed, and the veterans are reported to be agitating to set it up again as a cult-focus for the murdered dictator.
